data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer↔consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

File-disjointness: 8937 file_impact = _snapshot_schema.py (new), tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py (new), fetcher.py (producer validates vs schema). Disjoint from every sibling — fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). fetcher.py is solely 8937's; it is only READ by fe57's fake through fetch_snapshot. 8937 declares depends_on fe57.

7-mode walk:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 8937's three files are net-new or solely-owned. FakeAcliClient/fixtures are PRODUCED by fe57, CONSUMED (read) by 8937 via fetch_snapshot — clean producer/consumer split over the declared depends_on fe57 edge; no shared mutable write co-edited.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins the inbound read-side shapes (comment nested / issuelinks / parent / scalars); fe57 AC2 (fake serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation) feeds exactly those — mutually reinforcing with 59ef (return-shape fidelity) and 822a (behavioral round-trip of the same shapes). The outbound labels_applied list-vs-count conflict tracked on 33d0↔2359 is outbound-telemetry-internal; 8937's read-side snapshot schema neither asserts nor depends on labels_applied, so it is untouched.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937's only input dependency (fe57's fixtures/fake) is declared. Applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359), fixture capture (→fe57) are explicitly scoped OUT, consistent with the roster. The round-trip "…→ applier" binds to "the production path" abstractly — against current code the batch path is already production and carries comment/link/parent dispatch, and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction — so no hard 33d0-before-8937 edge is required for the test to be meaningful (same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for 822a). No missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. Four stories drive "the production path" from distinct angles on distinct files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip; 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity; 33d0 = outbound-only multi-sub-op APPLICATION correctness; 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP. Complementary, not duplicative; 8937 declares the others OUT. The documented anti-change-detector guardrail is assigned to 8937 by NAME ("owned by story B") and by 8937's own description + AC5 — an in-scope deliverable of THIS child, not an overlap.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937 does not edit the applier files, so it is not entangled in the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with the epic's anti-change-detector posture / "no net increase in brittle tests." No sibling forced into a conflicting conformance.
- Residual references — BENIGN. Every schema field references a live producer/consumer surface (fetch_snapshot writes + inbound/outbound differ reads). 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier, but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION (batch) path, not the typed leaf, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

NOTE: the documented anti-change-detector guardrail is OWNED BY THIS CHILD (story B = 8937) per the parent AC's verbatim "(owned by story B)" clause + 8937 AC5 — an in-scope deliverable, neither a roster coverage gap nor a G4 cross-child inconsistency for this pairing.

VERDICT: no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. Findings list empty.
